name: mlba

metadata.name: mlba
metadata.summary: Utility functions for 'Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and Applications in Python'

documentation:
# Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and  Applications in Python

Collection of utility functions used in the book

> _Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and Applications in Python_ (First Edition) 
> Galit Shmueli, Peter C. Bruce, Peter Gedeck, and Nitin R. Patel
> (c) 2019 John Wiley & Sons, Inc. 

Code for the book and the used datasets are available on https://www.dataminingbook.com/book/python-edition.


## Installation
Use `pip` to install the `dmba` package from pypi (https://pypi.org/project/dmba/).
```
pip install dmba
```
Should this not work, for example when you are behind a firewall, download the package from pypi and install from file, e.g.
```
pip install dmba-0.0.14.tar.gz 
```

modules.name: mlba.graphs
documentation:
Utility functions for "Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and 
Applications in Python"

(c) 2019, 2024 Galit Shmueli, Peter C. Bruce, Peter Gedeck

functions.name: mlba.graphs.liftChart
documentation:
Create a decile lift chart using ranking and predicted values 

Input: 
    data: DataFrame with ranking and actual values
    ranking: column name for ranking (predicted values or probability)
    actual: column name for actual values
    title (optional): set to None to suppress title
    labelBars (optional): set to False to avoid mean response labels on bar chart
    ax (optional): axis for matplotlib graph
    figsize (optional): size of matplotlib graph

functions.name: mlba.graphs.gainsChart
documentation:
Create a gains chart using ranking and predicted values 

Input: 
    data: DataFrame with ranking and actual values
    ranking: column name for ranking (predicted values or probability)
    actual: column name for actual values
    event_level: outcome of interest for the actual values (default 1)
    type: classification (default) or regression 
    color (optional): color of graph
    title (optional): set to None to suppress title
    show_counts (optional): show counts of cumulative gains (classification only)
    ax (optional): axis for matplotlib graph
    figsize (optional): size of matplotlib graph

functions.name: mlba.graphs.plotDecisionTree
documentation:
Create a plot of the scikit-learn decision tree and show in the Jupyter notebooke 
Input:
    decisionTree: scikit-learn decision tree
    feature_names (optional): variable names
    class_names (optional): class names, only relevant for classification trees
    impurity (optional): show node impurity
    label (optional): only show labels at the root
    max_depth (optional): limit 
    rotate (optional): rotate the layout of the graph
    pdfFile (optional): provide pathname to create a PDF file of the graph

functions.name: mlba.graphs.textDecisionTree
documentation:
Create a text representation of the scikit-learn decision tree 
Input:
    decisionTree: scikit-learn decision tree
    as_ratio: show the composition of the leaf nodes as ratio (default) instead of counts 
    indent: indentation (default two spaces)
Taken from scikit-learn documentation

modules.name: mlba.metric
documentation:
Utility functions for "Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and 
Applications in Python"

(c) 2019 Galit Shmueli, Peter C. Bruce, Peter Gedeck

functions.name: mlba.metric.adjusted_r2_score
documentation:
calculate adjusted R2
Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values
    model: predictive model

functions.name: mlba.metric.AIC_score
documentation:
calculate Akaike Information Criterion (AIC) 
Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values
    model (optional): predictive model
    df (optional): degrees of freedom of model

One of model or df is requried

functions.name: mlba.metric.BIC_score
documentation:
calculate Schwartz's Bayesian Information Criterion (AIC) 
Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values
    model: predictive model
    df (optional): degrees of freedom of model

functions.name: mlba.metric.regressionSummary
documentation:
print regression performance metrics 

Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values

functions.name: mlba.metric.regressionMetrics
documentation:
calculate and return regression performance metrics 

Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values
Output:
    dictionary of regression metrics

functions.name: mlba.metric.classificationSummary
documentation:
Print a summary of classification performance

Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values
    class_names (optional): list of class names

functions.name: mlba.metric.classificationMetrics
documentation:
Calculate and return classification metrics

Input:
    y_true: actual values
    y_pred: predicted values

modules.name: mlba.featureSelection
documentation:
Utility functions for "Data Mining for Business Analytics: Concepts, Techniques, and 
Applications in Python"

(c) 2019, 2024 Galit Shmueli, Peter C. Bruce, Peter Gedeck

functions.name: mlba.featureSelection.exhaustive_search
documentation:
Variable selection using backward elimination 

Input:
    variables: complete list of variables to consider in model building
    train_model: function that returns a fitted model for a given set of variables
    score_model: function that returns the score of a model; better models have lower scores

Returns:
    List of best subset models for increasing number of variables

functions.name: mlba.featureSelection.backward_elimination
documentation:
Variable selection using backward elimination 

Input:
    variables: complete list of variables to consider in model building
    train_model: function that returns a fitted model for a given set of variables
    score_model: function that returns the score of a model; better models have lower scores

Returns:
    (best_model, best_variables)

functions.name: mlba.featureSelection.forward_selection
documentation:
Variable selection using forward selection 

Input:
    variables: complete list of variables to consider in model building
    train_model: function that returns a fitted model for a given set of variables
    score_model: function that returns the score of a model; better models have lower scores

Returns:
    (best_model, best_variables)

functions.name: mlba.featureSelection.stepwise_selection
documentation:
Variable selection using forward and/or backward selection 

Input:
    variables: complete list of variables to consider in model building
    train_model: function that returns a fitted model for a given set of variables
    score_model: function that returns the score of a model; better models have lower scores
    direction: use it to limit stepwise selection to either 'forward' or 'backward'

Returns:
    (best_model, best_variables)

functions.name: mlba.textMining.printTermDocumentMatrix
documentation:
Print term-document matrix created by the CountVectorizer 
Input:
    count_vect: scikit-learn Count vectorizer
    counts: term-document matrix returned by transform method of counter vectorizer
